meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Analytics Setup to Scale Your SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn how to implement professional analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value, data-driven client projects.
content.answer_block: To move beyond basic apps, integrate event-driven analytics. Use Antigravity to scaffold your data schema and Claude Code to implement the tracking hooks. By automating the capture of user interactions, you transform simple interfaces into insight-rich SaaS products that command higher fees and provide actionable growth metrics for your clients.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the UI is polished, the code is clean, but the app feels like a ghost town. You’re shipping basic CRUD apps that lack the pulse of real user data, leaving your clients guessing about their next move. This is the difference between a coder and a product partner.

True professional-grade SaaS relies on the feedback loop of data. By moving from static apps to analytics-integrated powerhouses, you stop just building features and start selling growth. We are going to bridge this gap using the raw speed of Antigravity and the engineering precision of Claude Code.

steps.title: Define Your Event Schema
steps.description: Identify the key user actions that signify value, such as 'signup_completed' or 'feature_activated'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable TypeScript event tracking schema for a SaaS application including user context, event name, and metadata properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a types/analytics.ts file with the generated event interfaces'

steps.title: Initialize the Tracking Provider
steps.description: Setup a centralized analytics service that abstracts the provider, allowing you to swap between PostHog or Amplitude without refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust analytics utility class in JavaScript/TypeScript that handles event batching and error logging for a production environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the analytics service in src/lib/analytics.ts'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Wrap your primary application flow with tracking hooks to ensure no user interaction goes unrecorded.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook useAnalytics that provides a trackEvent function, ensuring it automatically includes the current user's session ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the useAnalytics hook to the main dashboard container'

steps.title: Automate User Identification
steps.description: Ensure every event is tied to a specific user profile to enable cohort analysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function to identify users upon login, mapping their database ID to the analytics provider's user property format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate identifyUser into the auth callback'

steps.title: Verify Data Flow
steps.description: Implement a test suite to ensure events are firing correctly before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a simple test script to verify that events are being sent to the analytics endpoint without actual user data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a test script to package.json and run it'

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) as a custom event to show clients how fast their users reach success.
Use property aliasing to map database fields to human-readable labels inside your analytics dashboard.
Implement a 'debug mode' flag that logs all events to the browser console during development to avoid polluting production data.
Batch your events locally before sending to save on API costs and reduce network overhead.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your events; always hash user emails if you need them for cross-referencing.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics pipeline is live, set up a dashboard to visualize your 'Activation' metrics and share the link with your client to demonstrate the impact of your work.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering apps that provide measurable business value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
